regions.text: Hundreds of millions of people today can hear on the radio important statements by public men, statements which in earlier times could have been heard by few. It is a question whether at any time in history a man has been able to make his voice heard by as many as a hundred thousand people at the same time. Without the telephone and the radio a man can be heard only as far as his voice can carry.
New instruments are making men’s homes less private and public men more public.
whether: if or not. history: man’s past as we know it, the account or story of it.
voice: sound made through the mouth; the power to make such sounds.
